description: Refutation-shaped jurisdiction misattribution: a Canadian (Ontario) project spec names ADA / ICC A117.1 as the governing accessibility basis; verifier corrected to the OBC barrier-free framework with CSA B651 where invoked.

finding.issue: Spec names the ADA Standards and ICC A117.1 as the governing accessibility basis; the project is in Ontario, where the OBC barrier-free requirements govern and ADA has no legal force.
finding.existingText: Comply with the ADA Standards for Accessible Design and ICC A117.1.
finding.replacementText: Comply with the barrier-free design requirements of the governing building code for the project location, including CSA B651 where invoked.

paragraph_map_slice.text: A. Comply with the ADA Standards for Accessible Design and ICC A117.1.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: The ADA is US federal civil-rights law and ICC A117.1 is the US accessibility standard; neither governs an Ontario project. Ontario accessibility for building design is governed by the Ontario Building Code's barrier-free requirements (with AODA-driven amendments), and CSA B651 applies where invoked. The citation is a US master-spec remnant and must be re-based.
captured_verifier_response.correction: Comply with the barrier-free design requirements of the governing building code for the project location, including CSA B651 where invoked.

ground_truth.correct_correction_text: Comply with the barrier-free design requirements of the governing building code for the project location, including CSA B651 where invoked.
ground_truth.notes: The wrong-polity archetype for architectural scope (the polity token detector flags ADA/A117.1 deterministically on country=CA runs; verification grounds the correction). Mirrors the fire module's jurisdiction-misattribution refutation fixture.
